channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Authentication issues related to API requests
item.description: Jun 10, 15:27 UTC Update - We are investigating issues related to sporadic authentication failures impacting approximately 15% of API traffic. We will continue to investigate and provide updates. Jun 10, 15:27 UTC Update - Issues is experiencing degraded performance. We are continuing to...

item.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, and SAP Release Patches for Multiple Critical Vulnerabilities
item.description: Fortinet, Ivanti, and SAP have released security updates to address multiple critical security vulnerabilities that could result in arbitrary code execution and information disclosure. The security flaw patched by Fortinet relates to a command injection vulnerability in FortiSandbox, FortiSandbox...

item.title: Unpatched Langflow Flaw CVE-2026-5027 Exploited for Unauthenticated RCE
item.description: A high-severity unpatched security flaw in Langflow, an open-source low-code platform to build artificial intelligence (AI) applications, has come under active exploitation in the wild, according to findings from VulnCheck. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-5027 (CVSS score: 8.8), a case of...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Haiku 4.5
item.description: Jun 10, 14:59 UTC Update - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue. Jun 10, 13:19 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 10, 13:06 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
item.description: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues...

item.title: Infostealers Turn Millions of Devices Into Credential Theft Machines
item.description: As attackers increasingly favor stolen credentials over exploits, infostealers have become a primary source of access for ransomware and other cybercrime operations. The post Infostealers Turn Millions of Devices Into Credential Theft Machines appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Cyera Raises $600 Million at $12 Billion Valuation
item.description: Cyera is positioned as one of the most valuable privately held cybersecurity firms in the world with total funding topping $2 billion. The post Cyera Raises $600 Million at $12 Billion Valuation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Aryon Security Raises $29 Million in Series A Funding
item.description: In the post-Mythos era, the company’s platform helps organizations enforce security controls across environments. The post Aryon Security Raises $29 Million in Series A Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: the “steroid olympics” and a safer Mythos
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The “steroid olympics” were a circus—and a window into our culture —Amit Katwala A couple of weeks ago, at a $50 million arena built in a casino...

item.title: Increased HTTP 526 Errors
item.description: Jun 10, 11:41 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 10, 10:50 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 10, 10:20 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 10, 09:36 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is...

item.title: The “steroid olympics” were a circus—and a window into our culture
item.description: Testosterone. Methenolone. Nandrolone. Human growth hormone and EPO. Meldonium, modafinil, and mixed amphetamine salts. Clomiphene, anastrozole, levothyroxine, and liothyronine. Patches and capsules, creams and pills. A whole galaxy of steroids, metabolic modulators, and synthetic hormones coursing...

item.title: LandSpace Successfully Launches Improved Zhuque-2 Rocket, Marking Milestone in Chinese Commercial Space Race
item.description: Chinese commercial aerospace firm LandSpace launched the improved Zhuque-2E Y6 rocket on June 9, delivering two satellites to orbit and advancing toward reusable rocket capability with the Zhuque-3 program.

item.title: Glass Substrates Set to Reshape Semiconductor Packaging as Industry Giants Pour Billions Into Technology
item.description: Glass substrates and glass interposers are emerging as key replacements for traditional organic substrates and silicon interposers in advanced chip packaging, with Intel investing over $1 billion and multiple players racing to overcome manufacturing hurdles.

item.title: Gaming Tablets Emerge as China's Hottest Device Category in 2026
item.description: Once relegated to video consumption and online learning, tablets have reinvented themselves as portable gaming consoles. A 150% surge in transaction volume signals the rise of a new device category driven by mobile gamers seeking bigger screens and better performance.

item.title: Tsinghua-Harvard Team's Acorn Robot Develops 'Zero-Data' Robot That Learns Through Instinct, Not Training Data
item.description: A team founded by Tsinghua and Harvard-educated researchers has created a robot that learns physical manipulation with zero training data, using only tactile sensors and instinct-driven trial and error to solve complex tasks like picking up a flat credit card.

item.title: Alibaba Partnership Committee Directly Rebukes DingTalk Management After Viral Resignation Essay
item.description: Alibaba's Partnership Committee issued a rare internal memo directly criticizing DingTalk's management culture after a former product manager's resignation essay detailing toxic workplace practices went viral across Chinese social media.

item.title: ICS Patch Tuesday: Vulnerabilities Fixed by Siemens, Schneider, Phoenix Contact
item.description: In addition, Rockwell Automation announced some enhancements to its SecureOT cybersecurity solution for OT. The post ICS Patch Tuesday: Vulnerabilities Fixed by Siemens, Schneider, Phoenix Contact appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Log Explorer queries failing
item.description: Jun 10, 07:34 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating an issue where a small proportion of Log Explorer queries are observed to be failing. More updates to follow shortly.

item.title: ServiceNow Flaw Exploited to Gain Unauthorized Access to Customer Instances
item.description: ServiceNow has warned about a security incident in which unknown threat actors exploited a flaw to obtain deeper unauthorized access to susceptible instances. "On June 5, 2026, ServiceNow applied a security update to hosted customer instances," the company revealed in an advisory that requires...

item.title: No Patch Planned for Exploited Arista EOS Vulnerability
item.description: Organizations are advised to apply vendor-supplied mitigations or discontinue the vulnerable devices. The post No Patch Planned for Exploited Arista EOS Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Microsoft Defender RoguePlanet Zero-Day Grants SYSTEM Access on Updated Windows
item.description: The anonymous security researcher going by the name Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse) has released a proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit for yet another Microsoft Defender zero-day named RoguePlanet. "The exploit is a race condition, so it's a hit or miss," the researcher, who published the...

item.title: Six Proto6 Vulnerabilities in protobuf.js Expose Node.js Apps to RCE and DoS
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged half a dozen vulnerabilities in protobuf.js, a JavaScript and TypeScript implementation of Protocol Buffers (Protobuf), that, if successfully exploited, could result in remote code execution (RCE) and denial-of-service (DoS) attacks. "In affected environments,...

item.title: Increased Errors for Durable Objects in the Denver and Chicago region
item.description: Jun 10, 04:25 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 10, 04:19 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 10, 04:02 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is aware of, and investigating an issue with Durable Objects which...

item.title: Business World Model
item.description: arXiv:2606.10044v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Businesses are increasingly adopting AI-enabled tools to improve productivity, reduce costs, and enhance products and services. However, the transformative potential of AI extends beyond automating predefined tasks: it lies in enabling intelligent...

item.title: Deployment-Time Memorization in Foundation-Model Agents
item.description: arXiv:2606.10062v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Foundation-model agents are increasingly long-lived systems that remember users across interactions, making memorization an explicit deployment-time function rather than solely a property of model weights. Existing work addresses parametric...

item.title: Exploratory Responsiveness and Adaptive Rigidity under AI-Assisted Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2606.10086v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper develops a theory of exploratory adaptation under AI-assisted optimization. The central argument is that the long-run adaptive effects of AI systems depend critically on how predictive assistance interacts with exploratory responsiveness...

item.title: Predictive Assistance and the Temporal Dynamics of Exploratory Compression
item.description: arXiv:2606.10094v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Classical theories of cognition describe problem solving as exploratory search through structured problem spaces in which repeated interaction gradually compresses search into efficient representational structures. Predictive artificial intelligence...

item.title: From Senses to Decisions: The Information Flow of Auditory and Visual Perception in Multimodal LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2606.10147v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) can listen and see, but how do audio and visual signals actually travel through the network to shape an answer? Despite their growing role in research and real-world applications, the internal pathways through...

item.title: Mechanistic Analysis of Alignment Algorithms in Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.09850v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Post-training alignment algorithms are predominantly evaluated as black boxes, obscuring how they reshape language models' internal computations. We present a systematic mechanistic analysis of six preference-optimization methods: PPO, DPO, SimPO,...

item.title: SynIB: Informational Bottleneck for Maximizing Synergy in Multimodal Learning
item.description: arXiv:2606.09853v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A central objective in multimodal learning is to capture synergy: task-relevant information that arises only from the joint use of multiple modalities, and is not available from any single modality alone. While most approaches operate at the...

item.title: Uncertainty-aware Multi-fidelity Closure via Conditional Normalizing Flows
item.description: arXiv:2606.09857v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reduced-order models (ROMs) provide an efficient surrogate for complex multiscale systems, but their predictive accuracy is often compromised by truncation errors and the inadequate representation of interactions between resolved and unresolved...

item.title: Mitigating Manifold Departure: Uncertainty-Aware Subspace Rectification for Trustworthy MLLM Decoding
item.description: arXiv:2606.09859v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: MLLMs frequently hallucinate objects inconsistent with visual inputs. This issue is typically attributed to the over-reliance on language priors, which can override the visual context. Recent training-free decoding strategies address this by...

item.title: Conformal Risk Prediction for Non-Alcoholic Fatty Liver Disease Using Gradient Boosting with Distribution-Free Coverages
item.description: arXiv:2606.09860v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) affects roughly 25% of global adults, posing substantial hepatic and cardiovascular risks. Yet, population-level screening tools remain inadequate. We present Method, a machine-learning framework for NAFLD...

item.title: Automated Scoring of Arabic Text Using Large Language Models: A Literature Review
item.description: arXiv:2606.09830v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In modern educational systems, Automatic Text Scoring (ATS) plays a central role by enabling scalable and consistent evaluation of learner responses without human intervention. Recently, the increased accessibility of LLMs and Arabic-specific datasets...

item.title: Can Multi-Agent LLMs Identify Their Peers? Stylometric Fingerprinting in Role-Constrained Political Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2606.09854v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent large language model (LLM) pipelines for political statement analysis are vulnerable to peer-preservation bias: models tend to protect peer models from deactivation and show identity-dependent scoring distortions. Prompt-level...

item.title: Using Probabilistic Programs to Train Inductive Reasoning in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.09856v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Post-training Large Language Models (LLMs) for reasoning typically focuses on deductive tasks such as mathematics and coding where correctness is verifiable. Yet, many real-world reasoning problems are inductive: agents must infer uncertain beliefs...

item.title: Less Context, More Accuracy: A Bi-Temporal Memory Engine for LLM Agents Where a Lean Retrieved Context Beats the Full History
item.description: arXiv:2606.09900v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-term memory is the missing layer for LLM agents: across sessions they forget, and the common workaround -- replaying the whole history into the prompt -- is expensive, slow, and, as distractors accumulate, less accurate. Most memory systems win...

item.title: BenSyc: Benchmarking Conversational Sycophancy and Human Alignment in LLMs for Bengali Contexts
item.description: arXiv:2606.10061v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly participate in emotionally sensitive social conversations, where responses may shift from balanced support toward excessive validation or escalatory alignment. Existing sycophancy research primarily focuses on...

item.title: [AINews] Anthropic Claude Fable 5 — Mythos but Safe, with Controversial Terms
item.description: The much anticipated launch of the Mythos-class model was marred by some controversial usage policies

item.title: UPDATE: Network traffic to Google Cloud originating from Delhi, Chennai, Mumbai and surrounding areas is experiencing intermittent periods of elevated latency and possible packet loss.
item.description: Incident began at 2026-06-09 11:22 (all times are US/Pacific).Summary Network traffic to Google Cloud originating from Delhi, Chennai, Mumbai and surrounding areas is experiencing intermittent periods of elevated latency and possible packet loss. Description A fire at a third-party data center...

item.title: Claude Fable 5 and new AI safety fables
item.description: One step further into the power politics of frontier AI systems.

item.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
item.description: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and...

item.title: Fable not available in the model picker in Claude Code Desktop
item.description: Jun 9, 21:03 UTC Resolved - This issue has been resolved. If you are experiencing issues with selecting Claude Fable within the Claude Code Desktop application, please ensure you have updated to the latest version. Jun 9, 20:16 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.6
item.description: Jun 9, 20:32 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Can Voice Agents Handle Bilingual Customers? Benchmarking Frontier ASR on Code-Switched Speech
Introducing North Mini Code: Cohere’s First Model For Developers
Fluid, natural voice translation with Gemini 3.5 Live Translate
item.description: Gemini 3.5 Live Translate brings near real-time, natural speech translation to Google AI Studio, Google Translate and Google Meet.

item.title: Hyperdrive and KV API errors
item.description: Jun 9, 14:37 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 9, 14:20 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare has identified an issue where some customers may experience latency or errors using Hyperdrive over Tunnels or the Workers KV API. We are working to analyze and...

item.title: Introducing Gemma 4 12B: a unified, encoder-free multimodal model
Powering the future of robotics in Europe
